diagrams.title: Perspective Projection with Vanishing Point
diagrams.description: Shows a 3D scene being projected onto a 2D viewing plane using perspective projection. Parallel lines converge toward a vanishing point on the horizon, demonstrating how depth is perceived in real-world vision.
elements: Projection rays from eye to corners
diagrams.purpose: Explains how perspective projection simulates human vision by making distant objects appear smaller and parallel lines converge at vanishing points

diagrams.title: Orthographic vs Perspective Projection Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing the same 3D cube rendered with orthographic (parallel) projection on the left and perspective projection on the right, illustrating the fundamental difference in how depth is represented.
elements: Left: orthographic cube with parallel edges
Right: perspective cube with converging edges
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the difference between parallel projections (used in CAD) and perspective projections (used in realistic rendering), helping students understand when each is appropriate

diagrams.title: Viewing Frustum and Camera Model
diagrams.description: Shows the 3D volume (frustum) that represents what a camera can see, including the near plane, far plane, and the relationship between the camera position, projection plane, and the view volume.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the viewing frustum concept, which is essential for understanding how 3D scenes are clipped and rendered, and how field of view affects perspective
